steps.title: Confirm scene safety
steps.description: Confirm scene is safe before approaching. Note hazards (traffic, fire, structural collapse, violence). Ensure adequate PPE including cervical collar and high-visibility vest.

steps.title: Assess mechanism of injury
steps.description: Determine mechanism: MVA (frontal, lateral, rollover, pedestrian struck), fall (height), penetrating injury, blast. Note vehicle intrusion, ejection, entrapment, or prolonged extrication.

steps.title: Primary survey (ABCDE)
steps.description: Rapid structured assessment: Airway (with C-spine protection), Breathing, Circulation (haemorrhage control), Disability (GCS, pupils), Exposure (full body check for hidden injuries).
steps.guideline_ref: Kenya BLS/ACLS Field Protocol — Trauma Primary Survey

steps.title: Apply cervical spine protection
steps.description: Apply rigid cervical collar and maintain manual in-line stabilisation until full spinal immobilisation is complete (long board, head blocks, straps).

steps.title: Control major haemorrhage
steps.description: Apply direct pressure, haemostatic dressings, or tourniquets for life-threatening external haemorrhage. Pelvic binder for suspected pelvic fracture. Pack junctional wounds.

steps.title: Establish IV/IO access
steps.description: Two large-bore (14-16G) peripheral IV lines in antecubital fossae. If unable to obtain IV access within 90 seconds, establish IO access. Begin warmed crystalloid infusion per local protocol.

steps.title: Record initial vitals
steps.description: Record full vital signs: RR, SpO2, BP, HR, consciousness (AVPU/GCS), temperature. Note any asymmetry in pupils or limb movement.

steps.title: Initiate fluid resuscitation
steps.description: Permissive hypotension (target SBP 80-90 mmHg) unless traumatic brain injury suspected (target SBP ≥100). Administer warmed crystalloid per protocol. Reassess after each 500ml bolus.

steps.title: Reassess vitals (every 5 min)
steps.description: Repeat vital signs every 5 minutes during transport. Document trend. Alert receiving facility of any deterioration (BP drop, HR rise, GCS fall, SpO2 drop).

steps.title: Determine disposition
steps.description: Record destination facility, transport mode (lights/sirens vs. routine), and handoff readiness. Ensure trauma team notification sent via dispatch.
